name: Spring Green Pasta
recipeIngredient: 6 Thin asparagus spears, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
1/2 cup Tiny green peas (if using fresh, thawed)
recipeInstructions:
Fill a mixing bowl with cold water. Add the juice from half of the lemon and the whole lemon into the water. Set aside.
Trim off and discard the top 1/2 inch of each artichoke using a sharp knife. Place the trimmed tops in an acidulated (water with lemon) to prevent browning.
Remove the outer leaves from the artichokes, leaving the tender green leaves. Gently pry open the artichokes and scoop out the chokes using a melon baller.
Cut the artichokes into three or four slices, dropping them into the acidulated water as you work.
Bring a large saucepan of salted water to a boil. Drain the artichokes and add them to the pan. Simmer for 10 minutes. Drain again and rinse under cold water. Set aside.
Boil the reserved artichoke cooking liquid and add the asparagus. Simmer for 3 minutes, then drain and rinse the asparagus. Set aside.
Bring a large pot of fresh water to a boil. Cook the orecchlette pasta until tender at a rolling boil.
In a saucepan, heat olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter over medium heat. Sauté scallions and garlic for 3 minutes. Add olives, peas, drained artichokes, asparagus, remaining butter, reserved cooking liquid, and 3 tablespoons Parmesan. Cook until heated through (2 minutes). Stir in pasta cooking water.
Drain the pasta and toss with the sauce. Season with salt, pepper, and additional Parmesan. Serve immediately.
